title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions Dominate Sentiment
subtitle: March 30, 2026 - Market Wrap-up

items.headline: US Markets Edge Higher in Volatile Trading Amid Middle East Conflict
items.summary: U.S. stock futures rose on Monday after a sharp sell-off on Friday, as markets began a holiday-shortened week. However, gains were pared throughout the day, reflecting persistent investor caution. The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly the US-Iran war, continues to be the primary driver of market sentiment, fueling concerns over inflation, stagflation, and crude oil supply. Brent crude surged towards $115-$116 per barrel. The Nasdaq and Dow Jones officially entered correction territory last week, with the S&P 500 also losing ground. Not financial advice.

items.headline: S&P 500 Performance
items.statContext: Up 0.1% on the day, after early gains of up to 0.8%. Index declined 2.1% for the week ending March 25, 2026.

items.headline: NASDAQ Performance
items.statContext: Slipped 0.1% on the day after early gains. Officially entered correction territory last week, falling 3.2% for the week ending March 25, 2026.

items.headline: Dow Jones Performance
items.statContext: Up 0.4% on the day, after early gains of up to 0.88%. Officially entered correction territory last week, declining 0.9% for the week ending March 25, 2026.

items.headline: United Therapeutics Surges on Positive Trial Results
items.summary: United Therapeutics Corporation ($UTHR) shares surged after nebulized Tyvaso (treprostinil) showed strong Phase 3 results in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, demonstrating statistically significant improvement in lung function.

items.headline: Iterum Therapeutics Doubles in Pre-Market
items.summary: Iterum Therapeutics PLC ($ITRM) more than doubled in pre-market trade following the announcement of a winding-up petition filing in Ireland.

items.headline: Aluminum Stocks Rally Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: Alcoa Corporation ($AA) rallied sharply amid escalating Middle East tensions after Iranian attacks hit key production facilities, raising concerns over prolonged aluminum supply disruptions.

items.headline: Dimensional ETF Leads Pre-Market Declines
items.summary: Dimensional - International Core Equity 2 ETF ($DFIC) saw significant losses in pre-market trading.

items.headline: Manulife Financial Corp. Among Top Losers
items.summary: Manulife Financial Corporation ($MFC) experienced notable declines in pre-market trading.

items.headline: Caterpillar Faces Steep Declines in Early Trading
items.summary: Caterpillar ($CAT) recorded one of the steepest declines among Dow components in early trading.

items.headline: Fed Chair Powell Addresses Rate Outlook Amid Economic Risks
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell stated on Monday that the risks to the U.S. economy suggest interest rates may need to be adjusted both lower or higher. He cited downside risks to the labor market, potentially warranting lower rates, and upside risks to inflation, suggesting against low rates. Powell reiterated the Fed's commitment to its 2% inflation target and noted that no imminent decision on rates is expected due to uncertainties surrounding the economic effects of the ongoing Iran war.

items.headline: Oil Prices Continue Ascent as Middle East Conflict Intensifies
items.summary: Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, surged as much as 3.7% today to $116.75 a barrel, putting it on track for a record monthly gain of approximately 60% in March. This rally is driven by the escalating Middle East conflict and concerns over disruptions in key shipping routes like the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Continues; Energy and Consumer Staples Show Resilience
items.summary: A notable theme in the market is a rotation away from technology stocks towards more value-oriented, cyclical, and defensive sectors. Energy and consumer staples stocks have shown resilience, with energy stocks being top performers due to soaring oil prices. Conversely, financials, consumer discretionary, and communication services sectors experienced declines.

items.headline: Rezolve Ai Reports Strong 2025 Results, Raises 2026 Guidance
items.summary: Rezolve Ai ($RZLV) announced robust full-year 2025 financial results, materially exceeding market expectations with reported GAAP revenue of $46.8 million. The global leader in Agentic Commerce also raised its FY 2026 revenue guidance to $360 million, citing explosive exit velocity from 2025 and achieving global infrastructure scale.
